description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly blue-gray coloration and visible white veining running diagonally across its surfaces. The stone has a rough, jagged texture with some angular edges and a partially hollowed-out section visible from one side, suggesting natural fracturing or weathering. Its surfaces display a matte, unpolished finish, and the stone appears dense and solid, with no significant artificial modifications. The stone's coloration is mottled, with subtle variations in blue, gray, and white, giving it a natural, geological appearance. A small, circular label or sticker is affixed to one of the flatter surfaces, indicating it may have been cataloged or collected. This stone would be commonly found as a natural obstacle or decorative element in urban parks, gardens, or along pathways.
description: A rough, blue-gray stone with white veining, jagged edges, and a partially hollowed side, featuring a matte, natural finish.
description_view_0: This view highlights the hollowed-out section and jagged, uneven edges, with visible veining and a rough surface.
description_view_1: This side shows a relatively flat surface with diagonal white veining and a more uniform blue-gray coloration.
description_view_2: This angle reveals the stone's thickness, angular edges, and the small circular label on the top surface.
description_view_3: This view presents another flat face with prominent veining and the label visible, emphasizing the stone's blocky form.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or natural barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Diagonal white veining and mottled blue-gray coloration, irregular and naturally occurring
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished with visible natural fracturing, indicating moderate weathering
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured or paved surfaces, but can blend with other natural stones or vegetation in landscaped areas
